issues: The draft says 'Steve Jaques penalty' led to Humboldt's power-play goal, but the OCR clearly states it was Tony Bobbitt who closed his hand on the puck (at 8:28 of the third period), resulting in the power-play goal that tied the game 3-3. Steve Jaques received a five-minute match penalty in overtime (3:02 OT), AFTER Gaber's winner, not before it.

The draft describes this as a preview article ('Abbott Cup Showdown Tonight') but the OCR is actually a game report covering game six (the 4-3 OT Humboldt win that forced game seven) AND previewing game seven tonight — the headline in the draft does not match the mixed game-report/preview nature of the article.

The headline 'Abbott Cup Showdown Tonight for Sockeyes, Broncos' may be inaccurate; the OCR does not clearly display this headline text, and the article reads more like a game six recap with a game seven preview.

The draft does not mention Tony Bobbitt's infraction (hand on puck), which was the actual penalty that led to Humboldt's tying power-play goal — a key factual omission that distorts the account of events.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Recap of Abbott Cup western final game six and preview of game seven, with Richmond Sockeyes facing Humboldt Broncos after a 4-3 overtime loss forced a deciding match on April 29, 1987.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Richmond Sockeyes and Humboldt Broncos were set to meet in game seven of the Abbott Cup junior-A western final on April 29, 1987, at Richmond Arena after Humboldt forced a decider by winning game six 4-3 in overtime on Mike Gaber's deflection at 3:31. Coach Orland Kurtenbach lamented two costly mistakes: Tony Bobbitt closing his hand on the puck, which led to Humboldt's tying power-play goal in the third period, and defenceman Steve Jaques receiving a five-minute match penalty in overtime after helmet-butting Bill McDougall. Paul Rutherford (twice) and Dave Tomlinson scored for Richmond in game six. Through six games the teams had played three overtime contests, with each club winning twice on the road, and Richmond leading the scoring aggregate just 21-20.
review.notes: The OCR headline text is not clearly legible in the extracted paragraphs — the draft headline may be plausible but cannot be confirmed from OCR. The most important factual error is the misattribution of the key third-period penalty to Steve Jaques rather than Tony Bobbitt; Jaques' penalty occurred in overtime after Gaber's winner, not before it. Editor should confirm headline from the scan image directly.
